message.content: {"items":[{"title":"Population Policy Acceptance Study (PPAS), 1988 – Federal Republic of Germany and German Democratic Republic","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12725","justification":"Nationally representative survey including both FRG and GDR with modules on fertility intentions, contraception, family planning, and attitudes toward population policy in the late 1980s; allows analysis of reproductive health–related attitudes in the GDR."},{"title":"Family and Fertility Survey in the German Democratic Republic 1988 (Family and Fertility Survey, GDR 1988)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10687","justification":"Microdata on fertility histories, contraceptive behavior, partnership histories and family planning among GDR women and men in 1988, directly focused on reproductive behaviour in the 1980s."},{"title":"Microcensus of the German Democratic Republic 1981 – 1% Sample","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10817","justification":"Large-scale household survey for the GDR with detailed demographic and family information (number of children, marital status, etc.) enabling study of reproductive patterns in the early 1980s."},{"title":"Microcensus of the German Democratic Republic 1985 – 1% Sample","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10818","justification":"Follow-up microcensus providing mid‑1980s demographic and household data including fertility-related variables (children ever born, household composition) for reproductive history analyses."},{"title":"Microcensus of the German Democratic Republic 1989 – 1% Sample","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10819","justification":"Late‑1980s GDR microcensus with demographic and family structure variables; useful for examining reproductive outcomes and childbearing patterns just before reunification."},{"title":"Reproductive Behaviour and Family Formation in the GDR and FRG, 1990 (Comparative Fertility Survey)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10938","justification":"Although fielded around reunification, this survey contains retrospective fertility and contraceptive histories for GDR respondents covering the 1980s, allowing reconstruction of reproductive health behaviour during that decade."},{"title":"Generations and Gender Survey – German Democratic Republic Subsample (Retrospective Data)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12208","justification":"Includes life-course and fertility histories for people who lived in the GDR, with retrospective information reaching back into the 1980s, enabling analysis of reproductive patterns under the GDR regime."},{"title":"German Fertility and Family Survey – East Germany 1992","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11635","justification":"Post‑reunification survey limited to East Germany (former GDR) collecting detailed retrospective fertility, contraception and partnership histories, covering respondents’ reproductive behaviour during the 1980s."},{"title":"European Fertility and Family Survey, Germany (East and West)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10937","justification":"Combined East/West German dataset with extensive reproductive histories; the East German component allows reconstruction of fertility and contraceptive behaviour in the GDR during the 1980s."},{"title":"Allgemeine Bevölkerungsumfrage der Sozialwissenschaften (ALLBUS) 1991 – East German Subsample","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4051","justification":"Contains demographic and family-related variables, including number of children and timing of births, for East Germans; responses from older cohorts allow indirect analysis of reproductive histories in the GDR during the 1980s."}]}
